sensoryProfile.sight: Not shown directly in prose; evoked through the chamber full of polished fools, clerks, ledgers, and the administrative pull waiting north of the patrol road.
sensoryProfile.sound: Implied council talk and civic proceedings rather than field noise.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Political gravity and institutional drag — the city as duty rather than comfort from Sylvanas's perspective.

establishedFacts.fact: Silvermoon is the northbound destination associated with the Convocation budget session and the day Sylvanas wants to avoid.
Lor'themar turns back toward Silvermoon to contain the political fallout of the border incident.
By chapter 19 Silvermoon is the city issuing formal recall through the Convocation, treating Sylvanas's continued field pursuit as desertion if she refuses to return.
Lor'themar has bought only a token mobilization and relay support to the northern harbors from within Silvermoon politics, at severe personal and political cost.

eventsHere.event: The Convocation session waiting in Silvermoon functions as the off-stage political counterweight to the field crisis.
Silvermoon becomes the off-page source of the recall Vereesa carries to camp, turning the city from abstract duty into immediate institutional threat.
